(a) What is your immediate response to the challenges posed in the above case? (b) Discuss the ethical issues involved in the above case. (c) Your company has been identified to be penalized by technological giants. What logical and ethical arguments will you put forth to convince about its necessity? (d) Being a conscience being, what measures would you adopt to maintain balance between AI innovation and environment footprint?
questions.subject: Case Study

questions.q: Case Study: Raman, a senior IPS officer recently posted as D.G. of a state with high graduate unemployment, learns that a new global terrorist group is recruiting unemployed youth from a particular community via social media and local communal organisations to carry out militant activities, with intelligence indicating many youth are already being radicalised online. (a) What are the options available to Raman to tackle the above situation? (b) What measures would you suggest for strengthening the existing set-up to ensure that such groups do not succeed in penetrating and vitiating the atmosphere in the state? (c) In the above scenario, what action plan would you advise for enhancing the intelligence gathering mechanism of the police force?
questions.subject: Case Study

questions.q: Case Study: Rohit, SP (Special Operations) in a naxalite-affected district, leads a successful cordon-and-search operation overpowering ten hardcore naxalites with sophisticated weapons, but more than five hundred agitated tribal women surround the village demanding their immediate release; with communication to his superior failing and two top insurgents carrying prize money and involved in a recent ambush, firing risks loss of civilian lives. (a) What are the options available with Rohit to cope with the situation? (b) What are the ethical dilemmas being faced by Rohit? (c) Which of the options, do you think, would be more appropriate for Rohit to adopt and why? (d) In the present situation, what are the extra precautionary measures to be taken by the police in dealing with women protesters?
questions.subject: Case Study

questions.q: Case Study: Sneha, a Senior Manager in charge of procurement for a privately owned hospital chain's new super speciality centre, discovers that her brother - a well-known medical equipment supplier facing financial difficulties - has submitted a bid; as committee head she is not bound to choose the lowest bidder, but awarding the contract to him risks charges of favouritism, while the management trusts her fully. (a) What should be Sneha's course of action? (b) How would she justify what she chooses to do? (c) In this case, how is medical ethics compromised with vested personal interest?
questions.subject: Case Study

questions.q: Case Study: Facing an exceptionally severe summer and acute water shortage, a District Collector deploys vigilance teams to stop farmers over-exploiting groundwater for irrigation; the agitated farmers allege the administration is anti-farmer and corrupt because nearby industries continue drawing huge amounts of water, yet closing the industries would render many workers unemployed. (a) Discuss all options available to the District Collector as a District Magistrate. (b) What suitable actions can be taken in view of mutually compatible interests of the stakeholders? (c) What are the potential administrative and ethical dilemmas for the District Collector?
questions.subject: Case Study

questions.q: Case Study: Dr. Srinivasan, a senior scientist heading a team developing a new drug against a rapidly spreading viral disease, faces intense pressure for first-mover market advantage; senior team members suggest shortcuts including manipulating data to exclude negative outcomes, selectively reporting positive results, foregoing informed consent, and using a rival company's patented compound, but he is uncomfortable taking such shortcuts even though meeting targets seems impossible without them. (a) What would you do in such a situation? (b) Examine your options and consequences in the light of the ethical questions involved. (c) How can data ethics and drug ethics save humanity at large in such a scenario?
questions.subject: Case Study
